content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (like ICM and RND from autonomous agent research) can be repurposed to prioritize which new unlabeled data samples an active learning agent stores in its long-term memory, creating a self-sustaining memory curation pipeline that outperforms static uncertainty-based sampling for open-ended active learning tasks. This framework lets the agent’s own innate exploratory drive shape its knowledge repository instead of relying on pre-defined sampling rules.
